description_long: This house features a rectangular brick exterior with a prominent covered front porch supported by two cylindrical columns. The upper level is partially open, revealing interior furnishings such as a bed and wall art, suggesting a loft or open-plan second story. The roof is flat and extends over the main structure, with a stepped section that adds architectural interest. Windows are arranged asymmetrically, with two on the front and two on the side, all featuring black frames. The main entryway is recessed beneath the porch, providing shelter and a welcoming approach. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on practicality and urban integration.
description: A two-story brick house with a flat roof, covered front porch, and asymmetrical window arrangement. The design is practical and urban, with visible interior furnishings on the upper level.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch with two columns, main entryway, and two front windows.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the extended flat roof, a side window, and visible interior wall art and shelving.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the uninterrupted brick wall and stepped roofline, with minimal window openings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing two side windows, the porch overhang, and a glimpse of the bed inside the upper level.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main door under a covered porch with two cylindrical columns and a flat overhang
